description: First-3-turn sequences extracted from real tournament play. Indexed by {deck_archetype, going_first}. Multiple-source matches strengthen confidence — entries with sample_count: 1 are observation, not yet canonical.

turn_1.supporter_target: search up to 3 Basic Team Rocket Pokémon to HAND (not bench)

items.purpose: tutor key Pokémon to hand; ideal pitch is a basic energy if any in hand
search a TR supporter (ideally Team Rocket's Ariana for T2)

energy_attach.energy: Team Rocket's Energy (special, counts as 2 energy)

turn_1.ends_turn_with: 1 active TR basic, 1+ benched TR basic, ~1 supporter banked in hand for T2 (Ariana), at least 1 special TR Energy attached

turn_2.supporter_value: draws to 8 if all in-play are TR Pokémon (5-card draw if not)
turn_2.ideal_secondary: evolve a Tarountula→Spidops if possible, then Spidops Charging Up to attach a basic energy from discard, then Rocket Rush attack scaling with TR Pokémon in play (typical 90-150 dmg early)

turn_3.primary_supporter: Team Rocket's Giovanni or Team Rocket's Archer
turn_3.purpose: Giovanni gust + draw 2; or Archer 5-card refill
turn_3.follow_up: Mewtwo ex Psydrive line: 1 charged Spidops bench + Mewtwo manual energy + special TR Energy = 160-220 damage range

entries.notes: T1 Proton-to-HAND (not bench) is critical — preserves Ariana scaling next turn. The biggest archetype anti-pattern: shipping TR Pokémon to bench via search instead of hand.

turn_1_or_2_setup.supporter_value: shuffle hand to deck and draw 8 — best when paired with Meowth ex 'turn-into-Lillie's' line

items.purpose: discard pitch fuels later Blaziken Charging Up + Crispin tutors

energy_attach.energy: Psychic or Fire (often via Crispin tutor)

turn_1_or_2_setup.active_choice: Budew → Itchy Pollen for item lock (NOT Phantom Dive turn) — slow archetype, stall while building Stage 2

turn_3_target.ideal_state: 1 Drakloak evolved, 1+ Torchic-Combusken line developing, 1 charged Dragapult prep, Munkidori benched for Adrena-Brain math
turn_3_target.first_real_attack: Phantom Dive turn 4 (or turn 5 if no Rare Candy) — pre-empts opp by gusting their unpowered key threat with Boss's Orders

going_first_variant.rationale: Choose to go FIRST despite slower setup, to deny opp's T1 Proton search — eat the T1 Phantom Dive ban in exchange
going_first_variant.trade: lose the T1 Lillie/Poffin tempo but gain massive disruption to opp's archetype-defining T1

candidate_lines_to_validate.line: T3 Giovanni gust → push opp big-attacker to bench → Mewtwo Psydrive on whatever's promoted
T2 spread Crispin energies across 2 Drakloaks rather than committing to 1 — defuses Boss's/Giovanni gust
